other_observers: Andrew Spencer, Cole Wild; See above
specific_location: Along Yellowjacket Creek, just north/east of the private land boundary, about 1/2 mile downstream from the junction with Risley Canyon.
description: Both birds were more or less the same; Andrew said he could see more red on the head of the male, but I never saw this. They were warblers, with short thin pointed black bills, slender bodies and relatively short tails. Plumage was pearly-gray all over the body, without any distinctive markings; no wingbars. Birds had faint whitish eye-rings. They also had dull rusty-red rumps, but these were difficult to see, as the birds mostly kept their wings folded over them.Behaviors: The birds were agitated, as though we might be near a nest, but we never could find a nest. They were moving very actively throughout the upper understory and lower canopy of the riparian grove, from 7-18 feet off the ground, occasionally foraging in the branches.Call: Birds were calling frequently in apparent agitation: a high, thin, very sharp, unmusical chip note.Plumage: alternate
